sourceMovie.title: Sister Act
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Sister Act works as a music movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The music and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sister Act clicked for you, Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in music and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sister Act?
faq.answer: Try Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit, Nick and Norah's Infinite Playlist, Victor/Victoria—each shares DNA with Sister Act while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sister Act?
faq.answer: Sister Act (1992) is best described as Music, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sister Act part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Sister Act Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
